params.name: The Garden of Arles
params.description: Van Gogh left Paris for Arles and quickly fell in love with the place. The nature of Arles drove him mad, and his creativity reached its peak. After entering the Saint-Paul asylum for treatment, he looked out from his ward window at the hills, clouds, and wheat fields, which inspired a series of nature-themed paintings, including The Garden of Arles. Van Gogh used vibrant colors and lively brushstrokes to capture the tranquility and vitality of the garden. Through these works, he not only presen …
